title: Reference design + dimensional spec: fraction tiles (halves-twelfths)
context: Fraction tiles are one of the four core manipulative families. Their defining pedagogical constraint is relational exactness: two one-half tiles must fit exactly on one whole tile, three one-third tiles must fit exactly on one whole, and so on. A dimensional error of even a few millimetres breaks the mathematical relationship the manipulative is meant to demonstrate. Before any PDF is generated, a reference design and dimensional spec must be produced and math-educator reviewed to guarantee correctness.
objective: Author a reference design and machine-readable dimensional spec for the fraction-tile family covering halves through twelfths, conforming to style-guide v1, and have it reviewed by a math educator.
acceptanceCriteria: Declares nominal physical dimensions (mm) for the whole and each denomination (halves through twelfths) such that they compose exactly within style-guide tolerance (the sum of two halves equals the whole within +/- 0.5 mm or +/- 0.5%, whichever is larger).
Specifies labelling that is unambiguous (e.g. '1/3') and uses non-colour-dependent distinctions between fraction families (shape, pattern, or label redundancy).
Specifies colour-mode variants (colour, grayscale, ink-saver) and large-print variant dimensions, all conforming to style-guide v1.
Includes the mandatory 100 mm calibration bar specification and conservative safe margins for A4 and US Letter.
Is reviewed by a qualified math educator for mathematical and pedagogical correctness; reviewer name and sign-off are recorded in the document.
resources: tasks/math-manip-style-001.json -- style guide v1 (units, tolerance, paper sizes, colour modes, accessibility)
tasks/math-manip-spec-001.json -- manipulative-spec schema to conform to
output: A reference design document (design/fraction-tiles-reference-design.md) and a companion machine-readable spec file (specs/fraction-tiles.spec.json) conforming to the manipulative-spec schema, licensed CC-BY-4.0, with math-educator sign-off recorded.
